decision_points.description: Use a proofreading high-fidelity polymerase for the GC-rich PCR attempt.

citations.title: Q5 High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor page positioning Q5 as a dedicated high-fidelity option for difficult PCR.

citations.title: Phusion High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor page positioning Phusion as a high-fidelity PCR enzyme suitable for demanding templates.

decision_points.description: Use a GC-rich PCR additive during at least one optimization attempt.

citations.title: Betaine improves the PCR amplification of GC-rich DNA sequences
citations.tier_justification: Peer-reviewed GC-rich PCR study supporting additive use for difficult GC-rich targets.

citations.title: The enhancement of PCR amplification by low molecular-weight sulfones
citations.tier_justification: Peer-reviewed cosolvent study supporting additive use for difficult amplification reactions.

decision_points.description: Use an extension time consistent with a 2 kb GC-rich amplicon on a modern high-fidelity polymerase.

citations.title: PCR Using Q5 High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor protocol stating a 20-30 second per kb extension range, corresponding to 40-60 seconds for the 2 kb PCR-01 target.

citations.title: Phusion PCR Protocol
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor protocol supporting the same order-of-magnitude extension range for Phusion.

decision_points.description: Use a cycle count within the cited genomic-PCR range.

citations.title: Protocol for Q5 High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor protocol using roughly 25-35 cycles, with genomic PCR typically using the upper end of the range.

citations.title: Phusion PCR Protocol
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor protocol supporting a 30-35 cycle genomic PCR workflow.

missing_gc_additive.canonical_diagnosis: A GC-rich PCR additive was missing, so the difficult template did not amplify cleanly.
acceptable_variants: No GC additive was used for the GC-rich template.
The reaction needed DMSO or betaine for the GC-rich target.

citations.title: Betaine improves the PCR amplification of GC-rich DNA sequences
citations.tier_justification: Peer-reviewed study directly supporting additive use for GC-rich PCR.

short_extension_time.canonical_diagnosis: Extension time was too short for a 2 kb amplicon, so the product truncated or failed.
acceptable_variants: The extension step was too short for the 2 kb target.
A longer extension time was needed for the amplicon length.

citations.title: PCR Using Q5 High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor protocol specifying extension time as a function of amplicon length.

low_fidelity_polymerase.canonical_diagnosis: A low-fidelity polymerase was used instead of a proofreading high-fidelity enzyme for a difficult GC-rich target.
acceptable_variants: Taq was a poor choice for this GC-rich amplicon.

citations.title: PCR fidelity of pfu DNA polymerase and other thermostable DNA polymerases
citations.tier_justification: Peer-reviewed comparison documenting the fidelity advantage of proofreading polymerases over Taq.

insufficient_cycle_count.canonical_diagnosis: Cycle count was too low for a robust genomic PCR band.
acceptable_variants: The cycle count was below the recommended genomic-PCR range.

citations.title: Protocol for Q5 High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor protocol providing the expected cycle-count range for genomic PCR.

overcycled_pcr.canonical_diagnosis: Cycle count was too high, increasing nonspecific amplification and smear.
acceptable_variants: Too many PCR cycles promoted nonspecific bands.

citations.title: Phusion PCR Protocol
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor protocol using a bounded cycle-count range for PCR to limit nonspecific amplification.
